turns.text: Q1 of fiscal 2025 was strong. Total revenues were $13.3 billion, up 7% in constant currency. Cloud revenue was $5.6 billion, up 21% in constant currency — above our guidance of 20%. Our remaining performance obligations grew to $99 billion, up 52% year-over-year, a record. OCI consumption revenue grew 45% in the quarter, driven by GenAI database services and compute instances. We remain on track for our fiscal 2025 cloud revenue target.

Oracle Autonomous Database with GenAI integration is seeing explosive adoption. We have signed contracts with over 40 AI companies to host sovereign AI infrastructure on OCI. We believe OCI is now the second largest enterprise cloud by many measures. Our multi-cloud partnerships with Microsoft Azure and Google Cloud are driving incremental customer wins and we expect these partnerships to generate billions in additional revenue in fiscal 2025.
Our next question comes from Brent Thill at Jefferies.
Larry, can you characterize the contribution from the multi-cloud partnerships with Azure and Google, and how much of the $99 billion RPO is attributable to those arrangements?

statements.claim: Q1 fiscal 2025 cloud revenue was $5.6 billion, up 21% in constant currency, above guidance of 20%.
Remaining performance obligations grew to $99 billion, up 52% year-over-year.
Oracle remains on track for its fiscal 2025 cloud revenue target of over $25 billion.
Multi-cloud partnerships with Microsoft Azure and Google Cloud will generate billions in additional revenue in fiscal 2025.
OCI consumption revenue grew 45% in Q1 fiscal 2025, driven by GenAI database services.
